free_text: Production of protons, deuterons and tritons on carbon
by intermediate energy neutrons
Main reference.
Contains numerical data.
Experimental site.
J.F., E.R., C.S., H.S., H.L.W.
J.E., Z.F., J.K., P.K., Z.K., Z.S.
Cyclotron at the Swiss Institute for
Nuclear Research (SIN). High energy neutron beam.
or p-C12.
12 cm thick beryllium target or 11 cm thick carbon
target, bombarded with 590 MeV protons. A proton beam
intensity of 120 uA gave a neutron flux of
7.0E+06 neutrons/sec within a well collimated beam
of 1 cm2. The neutrons were produced in 0.5 ns wide
bunches with a repetition time of 19.75 ns.
Continuous neutron spectra with a dominant 40 MeV wide
peak at 545 MeV neutron energy.
Each telescope consisted of two
thin (5 mm) NE 102A plastic scintillators and a third
one 30 cm in length. The solid angles were between
1.74 and 2.5 msr.
An additional counter array set at 45 degrees served
as beam monitor.
Used to obtain the neutron
intensity.
Graphite sheets, with thicknesses of 0.606 and
0.691 g/cm2 were used. For the intensity measurements,
a 0.583 g/cm2 sheet of polyethylene was used. The
targets were set inclined to the beam to minimize the
energy losses. The samples were placed at about 21.5 m
from the neutron source.
Corrections were applied to time drifts and for the
overlap of spectra from neighboring bunches.
Corrections were also applied to air background,
dead-time, and the detector efficiency.
Statistical errors. In addition, there are
systematic errors:
Uncertainty in calculating the
energy losses of the particles (2-6%),
Uncertainty in absolute normalization (10%)
Uncertainties due to approximations within the
applied model (5%)
Quasi-two-body-scaling.
IF.
IF.

Data for two Subents were restored from EXFOR archive.
DATA-ERR -> ERR-S. ERR-ANALYS updated.
The EN-mean values are the mid-points of six
neutron energy bins, each being 3 ns wide in the
time-of-flight scale.
ANG and E is the angle and energy, respectively, of
the outgoing charged particle.
